generated_headline: SOLD CUSTOMERS' REAL-TIME LOCATION DATA TO BOUNTY HUNTERS AND THROTTLED FIRST RESPONDERS DURING WILDFIRES
executive_summary: AT&T is one of the two dominant nationwide wireless carriers with legacy long-distance and fiber assets. FCC enforcement and journalism documented commercial sale of subscriber location data through third-party aggregators that reached bounty hunters and other unvetted buyers — leading to fines and consent decrees. During the 2018 Mendocino Complex fire, Santa Clara County firefighters publicly alleged Verizon/AT&T throttling on 'unlimited' plans hindered emergency coordination; the episode crystallized net-neutrality and public-safety arguments about traffic management policies.

tax.summary: Post-Time Warner transaction unwind and fiber capex cycles move effective rates; verify 10-K annually. Historic state incentive battles for call centers and towers continue.

legal.summary: FCC imposed fines tied to location-data sales/tracing failures after reporting revealed sensitive realtime CPNI reached gray-market buyers. Net-neutrality and throttling policies drew FTC/FCC attention across administrations. Routine tower siting and environmental reviews affect local litigation.

labor.summary: Wireless retail and field tech roles are mixed union/non-union; CWA represents segments of wireline and wireless workforces — strike and contract headlines recur.

environmental.summary: Lead-sheathed telecom cables and pole wood treatment historically pose soil/groundwater issues in investigative reporting; modern fiber build energy use and fleet fuel appear in sustainability reporting.

political.summary: AT&T is a perennial top federal lobbying spender on spectrum auctions, Huawei/Section 889 supply-chain rules, privacy preemption, and merger retrospectives — state capitals see franchise and small-cell preemption fights.

executives.summary: Leadership transitions after WarnerMedia spin reflect portfolio refocus on connectivity; compensation remains large relative to retail-sales hourly workers.

connections.summary: AT&T shares top institutional holders with Verizon and T-Mobile — convergent policy preferences on privacy federalization. Vendor ecosystems (Ericsson/Nokia gear cycles) tie carriers to defense-adjacent supply chains. Historic aggregator partnerships linked mobile carriers to data brokers later sanctioned.

allegations.disclaimer: The following are allegations and unproven claims. They are documented in credible sources but have not been adjudicated.
allegations.summary: Some legislators alleged under-compliance with spirit of CPNI rules prior to headline fines. Community groups alleged digital redlining in fiber prioritization; investigate per city datasets.

health_record.summary: Throttling emergency responder data during wildfire operations — as alleged in sworn public agency statements — can impair coordination latency in acute public-health emergencies. Chronic exposure to misleading 'unlimited' marketing intersects stress and financial strain when households overbuy capacity.

cheaper: MVNOs on AT&T/Verizon/T-Mobile radios with clear deprioritization disclosures
Municipal open-access fiber where deployed — separate ISP retail from dark fiber
healthier: Support public-safety priority policies codifying no-throttle classes for verified first-responder APNs
Advocate stronger data-broker elimination at network edge
diy: Faraday pouches for paranoia-grade location privacy; VPN does not fix carrier-grade SIM leakage
Lobby state AGs on location-data civil penalties

timeline.event: DIRECTV acquisition era begins (later unwound/spun — verify current portfolio)
Mendocino Complex fire — Santa Clara County Fire public throttling complaint gains national attention
FCC fines major carriers on location-data chain failures after investigative reporting

community_impact.displacement: Store closures and digital shift hollow out mall-anchor retail jobs.
community_impact.price_illusion: Bundled wireless-TV promotions mask equipment installment locks.
community_impact.tax_math: Small-cell siting deals trade ROW fees for coverage promises.
community_impact.wealth_velocity: Spectrum rents and subscriber ARPU accrue to Dallas HQ and indexed holders.

who_benefited.how: Dividend-focused telecom investor base historically rewarded AT&T yield
Low-friction resale of subscriber traces until enforcement closed lanes

who_paid.group: Abuse survivors tracked by bounty hunters via location data
who_paid.how: Human harm documented in investigative reporting
Throttling risk during disasters where microwave backhaul is thin

cost_absorption.the_gap: The tower flashes green — the map pin should never have been for sale.
